status_legend.vanmoof: VanMoof application code — reconstruct to C
status_legend.vendor-hal: NXP SDK / HAL / CMSIS (eDMA, M_CAN, peripherals) — vendor

functions.note: NXP eDMA: map peripheral instance base (0x40082000 / +0x25000=0x400A7000) to a 0/1/2 instance index for the channel-config lookup table
NXP eDMA: pack a Transfer Control Descriptor (SSIZE/DSIZE/NBYTES, CITER/BITER major-loop count, validates SADDR/DADDR alignment to the transfer size) into the channel's TCD words

NXP eDMA: configure/enable one DMA channel — checks the channel-interrupt bitmap at base+0x30, sets per-channel cfg at base+0x400+ch*0x10, calls the TCD packer, returns error code 0x1388 if channel busy
NXP HAL: read a driver/peripheral control-block state byte (offset 6 == 3) — predicate used by the DMA-callback dispatchers
NXP HAL DMA-callback dispatch: selects one of three handler pointers based on FUN_00000c14 state and AIPS reg 0x40000FFC low nibble, then tail-calls it
HAL: register a (callback,arg) pair into the first free slot of an 8-entry callback table
FreeRTOS: increment a scheduler/missed-tick counter (xPendedTicks/uxSchedulerSuspended-style ++)

functions.name: prvSwitchTimerLists
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer service: reload pxCurrentTimerList head — sets next-expiry to list head item value or 0xffffffff when empty
FreeRTOS: scheduler-state query returning 0/1/2 (e.g. taskSCHEDULER_RUNNING/NOT_STARTED/SUSPENDED) from two kernel globals

functions.name: prvInsertBlockIntoFreeList
functions.note: FreeRTOS heap_4/heap_5: insert a freed block into the address-ordered free list, coalescing adjacent blocks

functions.name: vPortEnterCritical
functions.note: FreeRTOS ARMv7-M port: disable interrupts, increment critical-nesting counter, DSB/ISB

functions.name: vPortExitCritical
functions.note: FreeRTOS ARMv7-M port: decrement critical-nesting (assert>0 trap), re-enable interrupts at zero
FreeRTOS stream/message buffer: under critical section, compute/validate available space and update read/write offsets (xStreamBufferSpacesAvailable-style)
NXP HAL: push a byte/word buffer into a peripheral FIFO data register at 0x40095000+0x08 (handles head unaligned bytes, word body, tail bytes)

functions.name: adc_to_temperature_lookup
functions.note: NTC/thermistor ADC-to-temperature lookup: scan a 166-entry uint16 calibration table (in SRAM) for the first step below the measured threshold, return temperature index (offset -0x28, clamped 0x7f) — battery/rail thermal sensing
NXP HAL DMA-callback dispatch (sibling of FUN_00000c50): pick default vs control-block handler at +0x10/+8 and tail-call with size 0x200

functions.name: prvProcessTimerList
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer service: process expired timers in the current list (call callback, reload auto-reload timers via vListInsert), handle tick overflow by switching timer lists
FreeRTOS timer service tick handler: under critical section, decrement timer-tick counter, on overflow re-insert all timers and run prvProcessTimerList, yield if needed

functions.name: prvProcessExpiredTimer
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer service: remove an expired timer from its list, re-insert into active or overflow list by next-expiry, flag yield if higher priority
FreeRTOS timer service: drain a timer object's two pending command/expiry sub-lists (counts at +0x44/+0x45) via prvProcessExpiredTimer, bracketed by critical sections

functions.name: vTaskInternalSetTimeOut
functions.note: FreeRTOS: add current task to delayed list — remove from state list, compute wake tick, route to delayed vs overflow-delayed list on tick wraparound (CARRY4), update next-unblock time

vTaskDelay-style blocking helper: asserts scheduler not suspended (DAT_158b/FUN_00005e68 = configASSERT trap), enters/exits via prvAddCurrentTaskToDelayedList, forces a yield (FUN_00001140 = portYIELD/taskYIELD path). Critical-section + delayed-list manipulation.
vTaskPlaceOnEventList helper: inserts current TCB into an event list (offset +0x18 into the queue/list struct) then yields. Small list-insert wrapper used by queue/semaphore blocking.

xQueueGenericSend: full queue-send with block-time, critical sections (FUN_00000db8/dd4 = taskENTER/EXIT_CRITICAL), event-list wakeups, priority-inheritance/disinherit handling, configASSERT traps. FreeRTOS queue.c.

xTaskNotify / ulTaskNotifyTake-class notification op on a notification-value word (param_1): test/set/clear bits, wake waiting task via event list, yield. FreeRTOS task notification (the extraout_r1 is a decompiler phantom).
xQueueReceive/xQueueSemaphoreTake: receive path with block-time, prvCopyDataFromQueue (FUN_00006144), event-list re-block, yield. FreeRTOS queue.c.

Flash/EEPROM-emulation page write-back helper: writes a 0x200-byte page (param+0x40 buffer) keyed by a 0xffff page-index sentinel, fills erased page with 0xff (FUN_00006ec8=memset), dispatches via a function-pointer table. Storage/NVM driver, likely SDK FTL/EE-emulation.

functions.name: pvPortMalloc
functions.note: FreeRTOS heap_4 pvPortMalloc: lazy prvHeapInit (0x5400-byte heap), 8-byte-aligned BlockLink_t free-list walk/split, xFreeBytesRemaining/minimum bookkeeping, alloc-bit high mask, configASSERT on misalignment. memset of payload via FUN_00006ec8.

prvUnlockQueue / queue-lock-decrement helper: walks list-item wakeups (the +0x2c/+0x48/+0x4c counters and 5-list-item priority math match FreeRTOS list/queue lock unwinding). Re-inserts items into ready lists.
xQueueGenericReceive (peek/receive into mutex-recursive-like struct): compares head/tail (+0x38/+0x3c), copies via FUN_00002a40, blocks on event list with timeout, yields. FreeRTOS queue.c.
Timer-command dispatcher (xTimerGenericCommand backend): commands <6 go to queue-send (FUN_00002bcc), >=6 to FUN_000067ee; checks scheduler state via FUN_00000cec. FreeRTOS timers.c command queue.

functions.name: power_send_mode_command
functions.note: Builds a 1-command message frame ([cmd-byte, 0]) into a bus buffer (FUN_00006226 acquire / FUN_00006a48 commit) and sends it; param_1 is the requested power-mode opcode. Device-command dispatch over the comms bus.

functions.name: power_send_capabilities_frame
functions.note: Builds a capability/limit frame: type-1 default {0x88,0xb8} or type-2 clamped to advertised max length/count fields (puVar3+0x16, +6); commits via FUN_00006a84. Protocol negotiation message.

Stream/message-buffer or queue trigger-from-ISR helper: under ISR mask (FUN_00005e68/e9c = enter/exit critical from ISR), transitions a state byte (+0x68: 1->2), moves a list item to a ready/pending list (5-item priority math, +0x2c/+0x18), sets a higher-priority-woken flag via param_2. FreeRTOS list/event wakeup.

Task version of the +0x68 state-transition wakeup (taskENTER/EXIT_CRITICAL via FUN_00000db8/dd4): moves list item to ready list, asserts ordering, requests yield (FUN_00005eaa). Counterpart to 0x329c. FreeRTOS list/event handling.

Lazy one-time init of a kernel object under critical section: seeds two 64-bit values (FUN_00005dfa), creates an object via FUN_00006dbc(10,0x10) and registers callback FUN_00000c94. Timer/daemon-task or event-group lazy init.

xTimerCreate + start: pvPortMalloc(0x28) timer control block (period, callback ptr at +7, flags), then issues start command via timer dispatcher FUN_00002d40 (cmd 1 task / cmd 6 ISR), pends PendSV by writing SCB ICSR 0xE000ED04=0x10000000. FreeRTOS timers.c (isCurrentModePrivileged/getCurrentExceptionNumber are CMSIS-port intrinsics).

functions.name: power_build_status_report
functions.note: Reads the live power/config state block (DAT_00003850) and packs many discrete rail/flag bits (offsets +0xd,+0x14,+0x16,+0x20,+0xa,+0x1b,+0x24,+0x1a) into a 2-byte bitfield message at *puVar9, tags it with opcode 0x2a3; on first use lazily creates a 1000ms watchdog timer (FUN_00003658) for the channel. VanMoof status/telemetry frame builder.

functions.name: power_log_event
functions.note: Event/status record emitter: zero-fills a 30-byte (0x1e) record, copies param_3 variadic args, sets event-id (param_1) and code (param_2), and publishes to the event bus/queue at [[*global]+0x590] via FUN_00006bd0. Diagnostic/event logging used throughout the power state machine.

functions.name: power_rail_enable_sequence
functions.note: Powers up a rail/peer with retry: clears an enable bit (global+0x20 ctrl word), then up to 3 attempts of {assert enable @global+0xe, settle FUN_0000158c(param ms), notify-wait FUN_00001844(...,1000)}; on success waits 500ms and logs event 0xac (attempt count), else logs failure 0xb2. VanMoof power sequencing.

functions.name: power_set_mode
functions.note: Power-mode negotiation state machine: target mode param_1 (mode 3 = special, skips rail enable). Up to 3 outer x 3 inner attempts: send mode cmd (FUN_00003200), log event 0xf6, wait for ack/notify (FUN_00001844, mode 3 vs others timeout 0x258/0x600), read achieved mode from state+0xa &0xf, compare to requested; logs 0xfe/0x109; returns 0 on match else -1. Core power_control state machine.

Generic transport TX dispatch: optional pre-send filter callback at obj+0x18; if it passes (or absent), tail-calls the lower send/driver fn at obj+0x10(obj, data). Thin two-layer driver vtable shim (comms/CAN HAL glue). Borderline app-vs-HAL but is library glue, not power logic.
CAN HAL filter/handler table walk: iterates param_1[0] entries of 0x2c bytes at param_1[3], invoking callback param_1[2]; returns matched index. Part of bxCAN rx-filter dispatch layer.
Wrapper over 0x5c3e: converts matched index to entry pointer (idx*0x2c + base[0xc]), -1 -> NULL. CAN filter lookup.
Entry guard wrapper for CAN filter lookup (null-check then 0x5c6a). Widely called dispatch helper in CAN/middleware layer.
STM32 bxCAN deinit/stop: via handle+0x160 clears regs 0xe0/0x54, sets 0xd4 bit0, requests sleep/init via reg 0x18 bit0 and polls bit0 (INRQ/INAK). ST bxCAN HAL.
bxCAN filter-bank enable helper: sets bit (id&0x1f) in FA1R-style array at base+0x20+(id>>5)*4.
bxCAN filter activation: calls 0x5cd6, checks filter master reg, sets TXRQ/control bit4(0x4) at mailbox reg +0x408. ST bxCAN HAL.
bxCAN status-flag accumulate: ORs ESR error bits 0x200000/0x100000 from handle+8 into out word. CAN HAL.

functions.name: vListInitialise
functions.note: FreeRTOS list.c: init list with 0xffffffff sentinel end-marker, self-referential head/tail pointers.

functions.name: vListInsertEnd
functions.note: FreeRTOS list.c: insert list item before list end marker, bump count.

functions.name: vListInsert
functions.note: FreeRTOS list.c: ordered insert by xItemValue (0xffffffff -> end), bump count.
FreeRTOS stream/ring buffer bytes-available calc (head+tail-... wrap). prvBytesInBuffer-style.

functions.name: ulPortRaiseBASEPRI
functions.note: FreeRTOS Cortex-M port critical-section enter: read BASEPRI, set BASEPRI=0x20 (configMAX_SYSCALL_INT_PRIORITY) if privileged, DSB/ISB.
FreeRTOS ring/stream buffer free-space calc (mirror of 0x5e56, -1); calls port assert-trap 0x5e68 on null handle.

functions.name: vPortSetBASEPRI
functions.note: FreeRTOS Cortex-M port critical-section exit: restore BASEPRI if privileged, DSB/ISB.

functions.name: vPortYield
functions.note: FreeRTOS Cortex-M port: trigger PendSV by writing SCB->ICSR (0xe000ed04)=0x10000000 PENDSVSET, DSB/ISB.
FreeRTOS software-timer / callback dispatch: enter critical (0xdb8), read timer node+0x1c callback at +4 and arg at +8, exit critical (0xdd4), invoke callback.

functions.name: uxListRemove
functions.note: FreeRTOS list.c: unlink list item, fixup pxIndex, decrement count.
FreeRTOS counting-object increment under critical section (event/notification or stream-buffer trigger-level update); asserts via port trap 0x5e68, uses 0x5e68/0x5e9c critical enter/exit.
bxCAN TX mailbox header field setup: packs DLC/control nibble into mailbox reg (mask 0xf0000, field <<0x18). CAN HAL.
bxCAN RX frame extraction: reads mailbox, reconstructs 29-bit extended CAN ID (& 0x1fffffff) from RIR bytes, copies 8 data bytes, invokes handler callback. ST bxCAN HAL rx path.
CAN multi-frame TX loop: chunks payload into 8-byte (DLC) frames, manages 3-bit/4-bit sequence counter, calls mailbox-write callback (handle+0x10) per frame. CAN driver/transport HAL.
prvWriteBytesToBuffer-style: advances stream-buffer tail with wrap then memcpy; FreeRTOS stream/message buffer internal

functions.name: xStreamBufferSend
functions.note: Stream/message-buffer send: privileged/ISR-context check, decrements waiting count, triggers PendSV via SCB ICSR (0xE000ED04); FreeRTOS

functions.name: can_request_obj_1a4
functions.note: Looks up CAN message object id 0x1a4 in the comms registry (FUN_00005c82), waits 100 ticks, returns its payload pointer; VanMoof CAN request wrapper

functions.name: can_request_obj_10a7
functions.note: Looks up CAN message object id 0x10a7 in the comms registry and waits 100 ticks, copies payload; VanMoof CAN request wrapper

functions.name: can_tx_task
functions.note: CAN TX worker: dequeues frames (xQueueReceive 500-tick), builds 29-bit extended ID frame (id&0x1fffffff|XTD), writes Bosch M_CAN message RAM mailbox + TX request bits; VanMoof CAN transmit loop
Stream-buffer write-with-wrap core (two-part memcpy across ring boundary, updates head); FreeRTOS stream/message buffer

functions.name: can_rx_read_element
functions.note: Reads a Bosch M_CAN RX FIFO/buffer element from message RAM (DLC->byte-count lookup, element-size (field&0x3fff)>>8) into a frame struct and ACKs it; borderline CAN HAL vs VanMoof RX driver — flag for review

functions.name: storage_erase_program_sectors
functions.note: Sector erase+program sequencer over 0x200-byte pages (calls erase FUN_0000100c per page, fills 0xff), validates id<0x3a and length; VanMoof flash/storage or firmware-update record writer

functions.name: can_build_event_record
functions.note: Builds a 13-byte CAN event/command record (id, type 0/1/2, optional 15-byte payload) and dispatches via instance vtable or FUN_000060b0; VanMoof device-command/event encode
Stream-buffer send-length helper: clamps requested length, writes length prefix for message-buffer mode then payload via FUN_000063f2; FreeRTOS

functions.name: xStreamBufferReceiveFromISR
functions.note: Stream/message buffer receive path: critical section (FUN_00005e68), reads bytes, updates waiting-task notify; FreeRTOS
Task-notify/queue-give wrapper: ISR-context check then notify (FUN_00002bcc / FUN_00005f68), triggers PendSV via ICSR; FreeRTOS

functions.name: xQueueGiveToSet
functions.note: Sends/gives to all members of a queue set and the set itself (iterates 0x1c-byte entries, count at +0x5c), then notifies; FreeRTOS queue-set primitive

functions.name: can_request_response_8808
functions.note: CAN request/response for message id 0x8808: gets a buffer from registry, copies 8-byte payload, re-submits and decodes the response via can_build_event_record; VanMoof CAN protocol RPC

functions.name: can_send_obj_1a4
functions.note: Registry lookup of CAN object 0x1a4 then encode+dispatch via can_build_event_record; VanMoof CAN send wrapper

functions.name: can_send_obj_10a7
functions.note: Registry lookup of CAN object 0x10a7 then encode+dispatch via can_build_event_record; VanMoof CAN send wrapper

functions.name: xStreamBufferSendFromISR
functions.note: Stream/message-buffer send core: privileged/ISR split, length-prefix for message mode, wakes reader and triggers PendSV; FreeRTOS

functions.name: xMessageBufferSend
functions.note: Public message-buffer send: assembles {len, hdr, payload} on stack and forwards to xStreamBufferSendFromISR; FreeRTOS message buffer API

functions.name: xStreamBufferGenericCreate
functions.note: Allocates message/stream buffer (count*size+0x48 header), inits ring head/tail and two waiting-task lists (vListInitialise); FreeRTOS stream/message buffer create

functions.name: memcpy
functions.note: Byte-copy loop (compiler/libc emitted memcpy)

functions.name: memset
functions.note: Byte-fill loop (compiler/libc emitted memset)
